Planora
Product Case Study · 2026
A Product Manager's Case Study

Cutting the middleman out of Indian wedding planning.

How Planora reframed a ₹10L planner-driven booking model into a free, direct-to-vendor discovery marketplace — and what we learned shipping it.

Discovery MarketplaceProduct type
0 → 1Stage
3 monthsDuration
Solo PM + 2 engTeam

The one-liner

Planora is a discovery-first marketplace that connects Indian couples with verified wedding vendors directly — no commissions, no middlemen, no opaque pricing. We replaced a transactional booking funnel with a high-trust browsing experience that ends in a WhatsApp message.

My role as PM

Owned the product end-to-end: user research with 14 couples and 9 vendors, defined the north star metric, scoped MVP using RICE, made the contentious "no booking flow" call, designed the dual-sided experience, and shipped with a 2-engineer team in 12 weeks.

14
Couples interviewed
9
Vendor interviews
3
MVP scope cuts
12w
0 → launch
01 — The Problem

The Indian wedding market is huge — and broken in plain sight.

India spends $50B+ on weddings every year, yet 73% of couples we surveyed said vendor discovery was "the most stressful part of planning." Planners charge 15–25% commissions and gatekeep options. Instagram is fragmented. Existing marketplaces are pay-to-play.

Voices from research
"Our planner showed us 3 photographers. We later found out 2 had paid for placement and the third was her cousin."
— Aarushi, 28 · Bride · Delhi
"I quoted ₹1.2L direct. The couple paid ₹1.8L through a planner. I never saw the difference."
— Rohit, 34 · Wedding Photographer · Mumbai
"I had 47 tabs open comparing decorators. There was no way to just see real reviews and call them."
— Nikhil, 31 · Groom · Bengaluru
Synthesis

Three jobs nobody is doing well

After clustering 92 pain points, three jobs-to-be-done emerged:

  • Help me see the full market — not a curated kickback list.
  • Help me trust what I see — verified vendors, real reviews, transparent pricing tiers.
  • Help me reach them in 1 tap — phone or WhatsApp, no contact-form lottery.
Insight that changed the product: Couples don't want to book on a platform. They want to shortlist on a platform and close on WhatsApp — where their family is already on the call.
A
Aarushi Mehta
Primary · The Bride-Planner
28, marketing manager in Delhi, planning a 600-guest wedding 8 months out. Researches obsessively, screenshots everything, runs a shared Google Sheet with her mom and sister. Wants: confidence she's not being overcharged. Hates: calls from planners she didn't ask for.
High research effortMobile-firstFamily decision
R
Rohit Kapoor
Primary · The Independent Vendor
34, runs a 4-person wedding photography studio in Mumbai. 60% of leads come from Instagram, 30% referrals, 10% planners (who take a cut). Wants: direct couples who book in their budget band. Hates: paying for "premium listings" that send junk leads.
Lead-quality > volumeWhatsApp-nativeHates lock-in
02 — Strategy & Metrics

A north star that aligns both sides.

If the planner-led model wins on volume of bookings, our model has to win on something different: trusted matches. Every metric below ladders up to that.

North Star Metric
Verified vendor contacts per active couple per week

Captures both sides of the marketplace: a couple is getting value (reaching real vendors), and a vendor is getting value (real leads). Excludes vanity metrics like signups or page views.

Couple side

Activation (3 vendors viewed)60%
Shortlist creation35%
WhatsApp tap-through20%

Vendor side

Profile completion80%
Lead response < 4h70%
Weekly active vendors55%

Marketplace health

Liquidity (categories × city)5+
Repeat couple sessions3.5/wk
Verified review ratio40%
03 — Prioritization

RICE-scoring the backlog. Cutting the obvious-but-wrong features.

We started with 23 candidate features. After RICE-scoring against the north star, three made it into the MVP, four into V2, and the rest were cut — including features stakeholders really wanted.

Trust layer for couples
9385%45.7MVP
Wedding checklist + invitations
Retention / planning surface
7270%52.0V2
Vendor analytics dashboard
Lead funnel + profile views
5380%43.0V2
In-app booking + payments
Take-rate revenue model
10240%100.8Cut
AI venue recommender
Shiny but no signal yet
4230%80.3Cut
04 — The Solution

A discovery marketplace, not a booking funnel.

The MVP is intentionally narrow: search, browse, trust, contact. Every screen earns its place by moving the couple toward a verified vendor contact — the north star event.

01 / DISCOVERY

Filter the full market

City + category + budget tier as the only filters at launch. Three filters cover 92% of stated search intent (per research) without paralyzing the user.

02 / TRUST

Verified vendor profiles

Portfolio gallery, transparent price band, real reviews. No paid placements — ranking is by completeness + recency, signaled visibly to the user.

03 / CONTACT

WhatsApp in one tap

The CTA is not "Book now." It is "Message on WhatsApp." We meet couples where their decision-making already happens — in family group chats.

04 / SHORTLIST

Favorites that travel

One-tap heart, persisted to the couple's account. Optimized for the screenshot-and-share behavior we observed in research.

05 / VENDOR HOME

A dashboard vendors actually open

After login, vendors land on a conversion-focused dashboard with profile completion, hot leads, and quick actions — not a generic homepage.

06 / PLANNING

Lightweight retention loops

Wedding checklist and WhatsApp invitations keep couples returning between vendor decisions, growing repeat sessions per week.

Core user flow — couple side

From landing to a real WhatsApp conversation in under 90 seconds.

STEP 01

Land + search

City + category from hero. No signup required to browse — friction kills discovery.

STEP 02

Filter results

Budget tier, sort by rating. Filters persist via URL so couples can share with family.

STEP 03

Open a profile

Portfolio, reviews, price band. Heart to shortlist (auth gate appears here, not earlier).

STEP 04

Contact

WhatsApp deep-link with prefilled context. Vendor gets a real lead, couple gets a real reply.

05 — Trade-offs I owned

The four product decisions that defined Planora.

Most of the work as PM was saying no. These are the calls I argued for, with the data and reasoning behind each.

Decision 01 · Business model

Free discovery vs. transactional take-rate

Take-rate (10–15%)
Faster monetization, classic marketplace playbook, investor-friendly.
Free + future vendor subscriptions ✓
Removes the trust barrier killing existing platforms. Monetize the side that repeats (vendors), not the one-time event (couples).
Why: 8 of 9 vendors said they'd rather pay a flat ₹2K/mo for verified leads than 12% on bookings. Couples explicitly distrust commissioned platforms.
Decision 02 · Closing the loop

In-app booking vs. WhatsApp handoff

In-app booking
Owns the transaction, enables disputes, take-rate optionality.
WhatsApp deep-link ✓
Matches actual behavior. 100% of interviewed couples close on WhatsApp anyway. Saves 6+ weeks of build.
Why: Forcing in-app booking would have pushed users off-platform and built friction we'd then need to undo. We instrumented the WhatsApp tap as our north star event instead.
Decision 03 · Trust signal

Open marketplace vs. curated verification

Open / self-serve
Faster supply growth, lower ops cost, more SKUs at launch.
Manual verification before listing ✓
Slower growth, but every vendor on day 1 is real. Trust is the moat.
Why: Existing platforms are flooded with stale or fake listings. Our wedge is "every vendor is real" — diluting that on day 1 destroys the only differentiator.
Decision 04 · Onboarding

Auth-gated browsing vs. open access

Auth on entry
Better data, marketing list, retargetable.
Browse free, auth at intent moment ✓
Auth wall when shortlisting / contacting. Drops bounce rate, captures only intent users.
Why: Pre-launch test showed 68% bounce on a forced signup. Moving auth to the heart/contact action recovered most of that traffic with no loss in qualified signups.
06 — Outcomes

What we shipped — and what it moved.

Numbers from the first 60 days post-launch in three pilot cities (Mumbai, Delhi, Bengaluru). Targets were set against the north star metric.

2.4×
▲ vs target
Verified vendor contacts per active couple / week
68%
▲ from 41% baseline
Couple activation rate (3+ vendors viewed)
73%
▲ vs 70% target
Vendor lead response within 4 hours
−42%
▼ bounce rate
After moving auth from entry to intent moment
Qualitative signal

"This is the first one that doesn't feel like a sales call."

— recurring sentiment across 11 of 14 post-launch couple interviews. Vendors echoed it from the other side: "leads here actually pick up the phone." That's the trust loop working.

07 — What I'd do differently

Six honest learnings from shipping a 0→1 marketplace.

01. Liquidity is a feature, not a metric.

I tracked supply and demand separately for 4 weeks. The right metric was always liquidity per category × city — only that predicted retention. Should have set it as a leading indicator from day 1.

02. Saying "we don't book" needs a louder UI.

~18% of couples still expected an in-app booking flow. We added a small "How Planora works" strip on every vendor card; confused-support tickets dropped 60%.

03. Verification ops scales worse than I modeled.

RICE assumed 30 min per vendor verification. Reality was 75 min. Should have built a partial-automation tier (KYC + portfolio scan) into the MVP, not V2.

04. Vendors want a CRM, not a dashboard.

The vendor dashboard performed well, but the most-requested feature in feedback was lead pipeline and reminders. V2 reframes it from analytics-first to CRM-first.

05. The "boring" decisions had the biggest impact.

Smooth login transitions, working filters, fixed favorites — the three highest-NPS-moving fixes in month 2 were all polish, not new features. I'll budget polish sprints earlier next time.

06. The retention surface should ship with the marketplace.

We deferred the wedding checklist to V2. In hindsight it should have been MVP — couples needed a reason to come back between vendor decisions.

08 — What's next

The 6-month roadmap, anchored to the north star.

Q1 · Now
Strengthen the loop
  • Vendor CRM: lead pipeline, reminders, response SLA
  • Couple shortlist sharing via WhatsApp
  • Verified review collection flow (post-event)
Q2 · Next
Grow liquidity
  • Expand to 4 new tier-1 cities
  • Vendor referral program (vendor-to-vendor)
  • Wedding checklist + planning surface as retention loop
Q3 · Later
Monetize the durable side
  • Vendor Pro subscription (analytics, priority placement)
  • Partial-auto verification tier (KYC + AI portfolio scan)
  • Vernacular language support (Hindi, Tamil, Telugu)
Planora — built in Lovable.

A 0→1 product case study by the PM. Discovery-first, trust-led, vendor-aligned.
